title: dermatophyllum gypsophilum subsp. guadalupense
intro: Dermatophyllum gypsophilum subsp. guadalupense is a rare species of cactus native to the island of Guadalupe, Mexico, characterized by its small, round stems and vibrant yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of this plant include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dermatophyllum gypsophilum subsp. guadalupense are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to promote wound healing and reduce pain. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve the extraction of its sap or the use of its dried stems in teas or infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
